ops.text:
E34 LEAN RE-DISPATCH → CHECK-BACK (no arms run, TEST untouched, ~$8.57 true spend). My lean ruling's premise is REFUTED by measurement: the lean invocation does NOT just cut cost, it CHANGES THE JUDGE. Lean re-baseline on the fixed dev-50 = 0.74 vs scaffolded 0.84 (18% verdict drift); tool-strip alone costs 6pts (0.84→0.78), the system/user split 4 more (→0.74). The scaffolded judge runs an agentic tool loop (out=1220, some 4869 multi-turn) that is LOAD-BEARING for auditor agreement; lean is single-shot (out=270) and materially worse. So "scaffolding cancels in the paired comparison" was wrong — invocation affects the judge's DECISIONS, not just cost. BLOCKER B: even lean busts $18 (projected $61.85; GenRM 8-sample + 50 opus verifiers dominate; 2.8x per-call saving can't close a 3.4x gap). Lean cost/call $0.0293 vs scaffolded $0.0826.

DECISION: E34 HELD (no re-dispatch, no further spend) pending two facts — (1) E30.7's lean-vs-scaffolded equivalence verdict (already running; E34's data pre-empts it toward 'scaffolded-required'), and (2) a focused recon on how the PRODUCTION pipeline invokes the triage judge (cc_transcript.judge.structured_judge / run_verdicts): agentic-scaffolded or lean structured call, and whether the E33/E34 harness's spawnllm run/RunSpec path is faithful to production or over-scaffolded. That fact determines the faithful invocation, its true per-call cost, and therefore a UNIFIED portfolio decision on E33/E34/E35 — the plan's $80 for all three is ~6x under the scaffolded cost, so likely only ONE prompt-scoring experiment runs at full faithful cost (E33/E35 compete; E34 is the weakest bet — TTC is mostly variance-reduction against a SYSTEMATIC bias per E30.3). Recon dispatched.
